era: 1981-1982. Hardcore Punk. E=10. Capitol Hill, Seattle, Washington. Alternative Tentacles Records. Because This Fuckin' World Still Stinks (1982).
key_albums: Because This Fuckin' World Still Stinks (1982, Alternative Tentacles) -- E=10 peak. Short songs, maximum velocity, minimal production, directly confrontational. The document of Seattle hardcore founding generation at peak.

theory_nodes.concept: Seattle hardcore founding generation: Solger (355, 1979-1983, independent, first, purely underground) + The Fartz (356, 1980-1984, Alternative Tentacles, second, national distribution). Two bands, one founding window (1979-1982), one city. Together = the founding generation. The engine places them at consecutive degrees 355-356 as paired acknowledgment.
theory_nodes.description: Paired founding: Solger + Fartz = Seattle hardcore founding generation.

theory_nodes.concept: Duff McKagan biography: born February 5 1964 Seattle. Fartz = first significant band (bass/drums, early 1980s). Moved Los Angeles 1984. Guns N' Roses 1985. Appetite for Destruction 1987 (Welcome to the Jungle, Sweet Child O' Mine, Paradise City). Arena rock mainstream. Return to Seattle later career. It's So Easy (and other lies) 2011. Full genealogy: Capitol Hill hardcore → underground LA → mainstream arena rock → Seattle return. The path from punk origin to mainstream absorption to reflective return.
theory_nodes.description: Duff McKagan -- full genealogy Capitol Hill to GNR to Seattle return.

theory_nodes.concept: Alternative Tentacles significance in 1982: founded 1979 Jello Biafra San Francisco, Dead Kennedys home label, national distribution network. Being on AT in 1982 = national distribution through same channels as DKs; credibility in underground hierarchy; community connection to national hardcore scene. Mechanism by which local founding = legible nationally.
theory_nodes.description: Alternative Tentacles 1982 -- national distribution, credibility, community connection.

theory_nodes.concept: E=10 calibration: output-to-capacity ratio, not output-to-volume. The Fartz: small clubs, small audiences, Alternative Tentacles distribution measured in thousands. But output per unit is maximum -- short songs, maximum velocity, directly confrontational, filling the acoustic and social space of what they are completely. Same calibration logic as Pacific Wren pairing: both fill their environment completely regardless of scale. E=10 where the capacity is fully realized, whether the capacity is arena-sized or club-sized.
theory_nodes.description: E=10 by output-to-capacity: filling the environment completely regardless of scale.

cross_references.relationship: Paired founding generation. Solger = first, independent, pure underground. The Fartz = second, Alternative Tentacles, national distribution. Together = founding generation Seattle hardcore.
Next degree. Sequence moves from Seattle founding hardcore (1980-1982) to Olympia riot grrrl (1993-1997). Tradition passed forward, geography shifted south to Olympia.

E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Both: maximum output from small source. Pacific Wren (10 grams, fills old-growth canopy completely). The Fartz (small clubs, small audience, maximum energy per unit). E=10 = output-to-capacity ratio.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. The loudest sound from the smallest source. Pacific Wren: 10 grams, highest volume-to-body-mass ratio of any PNW bird, fills old-growth canopy completely. The Fartz: Capitol Hill clubs, small distribution, maximum energy per unit. Both fill their environment completely. The intimate E=10.
